mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate financing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during low-rate periods are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9 percent signals severe labor market slack and collapsing consumer purchasing power, which forces corporate earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized expansion in consumer spending and residential investment, generating positive equity market momentum.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in both series fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over the subsequent 20 trading days, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion phases reflects robust manufacturing output and high capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and pushes S&P 500 returns higher.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations within the specified regime, invalidating the supply-side momentum link.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and triggers market expectations of restrictive monetary policy, causing immediate negative repricing of S&P 500 equities.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL values correlate with positive 20-day forward returns, or the rank correlation coefficient exceeds +0.05 over any consecutive 36-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150 thousand signal strong economic momentum and rising aggregate demand, which improves earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Payroll surges above the threshold consistently precede negative 60-day equity returns, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and moderate monthly inflation indicates healthy demand-pull price dynamics without economic overheating, supporting sustained positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects credit constraints and weakening supply-side momentum, which reduces corporate output expectations and causes negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Falling industrial production indices generate positive 5-day forward returns in over 65 percent of tightening regime observations, breaking the proposed supply-constraint transmission channel.
